author: EmilianoCervantes
text:
I followed the Sentiment Analysis tutorial: <https://cloud.google.com/natural-language/docs/sentiment-tutorial>

I set up a Cloud Natural Language API project, the credentials.

Next I run "pip install --upgrade virtualenv" and finally I installed Google Cloud Client Library for Python with "pip install --upgrade google-cloud-language".

I copy-pasted the first code """Demonstrates how to make a simple call to the Natural Language API."""  
It immediately marked an error.  
[![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11279332/37434533-7c36e6e6-27a5-11e8-8260-c1d4f69dc831.png)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11279332/37434533-7c36e6e6-27a5-11e8-8260-c1d4f69dc831.png)

And it was the only one. Does anyone know if I have to upgrade something? (Although I would find it strange as I just installed everything the tutorial asked for)

I'm currently on Windows 10, the other imports seem fine, I don't get errors from anything else.

author: EmilianoCervantes
text: I think is more of a problem with Visual Studio Code. Originally I ran it with the debug function of VSC, now I'm trying with the cmd and it executes the sentiment-analysis.py.

author: EmilianoCervantes
text:
But now that I run my app.

I opened a terminal, then run "gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file="C:...\credentialsFile.json"  
Next "set GOOGLE\_APPLICATION\_CREDENTIALS="C:...\credentialsFile.json"  
And then I ran the app but got this new issue:

File "C:\Users\ovman\anaconda3\lib\site-packages\google\auth\_default.py", line 67, in \_load\_credentials\_from\_file  
'File {} was not found.'.format(filename))

google.auth.exceptions.DefaultCredentialsError: File "C:...\credentialsFile.json" was not found.

author: chemelnucfin
text:
Hello, I'm going to close this issue. If you are still encountering problems, please do let us know.

Thanks.
